meaning: adj.崇拜偶像的,盲目崇拜的
examples: We can at least remove anything idolatrous from our own personal life.
They are minded, also, to establish the idolatrous forms of English Episcopacy.
The Persians were also idolatrous , so they replaced Babylon's gods with their own.
They regarded such shrines as idolatrous and thus prohibited by their strict interpretation of Islam.
It is out of the idolatrous dotings of the old Egyptians upon broiled ibis and roasted river horse, that you see the mummies of those creatures in their huge bake-houses the pyramids.
